constraints.label: High Volume
constraints.description: Must handle large volumes of data or requests at scale, often with burst capacity requirements

constraints.label: PCI Compliance
constraints.description: Must meet PCI DSS requirements for systems that store, process, or transmit payment card data

constraints.label: Low Latency
constraints.description: Must respond with minimal delay, typically with p99 response times under 100ms

constraints.label: Global Accessibility
constraints.description: Must serve users or devices across multiple continents with acceptable performance

constraints.label: Multi-Region Resilience
constraints.description: Must remain operational and available even if an entire AWS region becomes unavailable

constraints.label: Cost Sensitive
constraints.description: Must minimise infrastructure spend, particularly important for variable or unpredictable workloads

constraints.label: Near Real-Time
constraints.description: Must process and react to data within seconds of ingestion, not minutes or hours

constraints.label: Operational Simplicity
constraints.description: Must minimise operational overhead, preferring managed services over self-managed infrastructure
